sections.title: The Human Skull
points: The skull is made of 22 separate bones.
Cranium: The 'Brain Box' part of the skull that protects the brain.
Mandible: The lower jawbone. It is the only movable bone in the skull.
Maxilla: The upper jawbone. Unlike the mandible, it is fixed and does not move.
Sutures: The fixed, jagged joints that hold skull bones together for maximum protection.
Fontanelles: The 'soft spots' in a baby's skull that allow for brain growth and easier birth.

sections.title: The Backbone (Vertebral Column)
points: It forms the central axis of the body and supports the head.
Protection: It surrounds and protects the Spinal Cord.
Structure: Consists of 33 small bones (in infants) which fuse into 26 bones in adults.
Intervertebral Discs: Pads of cartilage between vertebrae that act as shock absorbers and prevent bones from rubbing.

sections.title: The 5 Regions of the Spine
points: 2. Thoracic (Chest): 12 bones. The ribs attach here to form the chest cavity.
3. Lumbar (Lower Back): 5 bones. These are the largest/thickest to support body weight.
4. Sacral (Pelvis): 5 fused vertebrae (the Sacrum) that attach to the hip bones.

sections.text: The spine is S-shaped! This curvature helps the body maintain balance and absorb mechanical shocks while walking or jumping.

sections.title: Skull & Spine Protection Summary
points: The Foramen Magnum: The large hole at the base of the skull where the spinal cord exits.
The Neural Canal: The continuous tunnel through the vertebrae for the spinal cord.

cards.fact: Atlas supports the head (Yes); Axis rotates the head (No).
cards.mnemonic: Atlas holds the world; Axis turns the wheel!

cards.fact: All mammals (even giraffes) have 7 neck vertebrae.
Cartilage discs between vertebrae prevent friction and absorb shock.
cards.mnemonic: Cartilage is the Cushion for your back!
